content:
Hi, I programmed some i2c PoE chip Linux drivers for my job and programmed quite some embedded rust. I would like to port a driver to rust. From what I understand, the encapsulations we are allowed to use are in rust/kernel. While I can find quite some stuff there, I can't find regmap or i2c/smbus abstractions. Is there a reason why it is not there apart from "someone hasn't done it yet"? Maybe to hard? Incompatible with Rust? What is keeping a very junior programmer with the bare minimum Linux driver and rust experience to try to encapsulate it and write a driver with it?
As you might guess, I probably don't have 2% of your technical skills, but I'm a bit tired of not contributing because I can't find a starting point

Is there a reason why it is not there apart from "someone hasn't done it yet"?

This is probably the reason. We are missing a lot of abstractions as nobody had a need for it yet.
If you would like to give it a try, feel free to.
https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/pull/264 will implement an abstraction for SPI. If it's kernel api is similar to i2c maybe taking a look at it would help?
Okay thanks for that! I'll see if I'm able to get a regmap abstraction going ^^
It looks like I will write a driver for my work which depends on i2c. Is there any work ongoing (did not find a pr regarding i2c)?
